title: React.js
titleLarge: Build dynamic user interfaces with React.js - A powerful JavaScript library for modern web development
desc: React.js is a declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It lets you compose complex UIs from small and isolated pieces of code called components.
shortDesc: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces with component-based architecture.
desc1: React makes it painless to create interactive UIs. Design simple views for each state in your application, and React will efficiently update and render just the right components when your data changes.
desc2: React allows you to build encapsulated components that manage their own state, then compose them to make complex UIs. Since component logic is written in JavaScript instead of templates, you can easily pass rich data through your app and keep state out of the DOM.
desc3: React has been designed from the start for gradual adoption, and you can use as little or as much React as you need. Whether you want to get a taste of React, add some interactivity to a simple HTML page, or start a complex React-powered app.

title: Node.js
titleLarge: Server-side JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine for scalable network applications
desc: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. It uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient.
shortDesc: JavaScript runtime for building fast and scalable server-side applications.
desc1: Node.js enables JavaScript to be used for server-side scripting, and runs scripts server-side to produce dynamic web page content before the page is sent to the user's web browser.
desc2: Node.js has a unique advantage because millions of frontend developers that write JavaScript for the browser are now able to write the server-side code in addition to the client-side code without the need to learn a completely different language.
desc3: Node.js provides a rich library of various JavaScript modules which simplifies the development of web applications using Node.js to a great extent.

title: Next.js
titleLarge: The React framework for production with server-side rendering and static site generation
desc: Next.js is a React framework that enables functionality such as server-side rendering and generating static websites. It provides a great developer experience with features like automatic code splitting.
shortDesc: Production-ready React framework with SSR and static generation capabilities.
desc1: Next.js gives you the best developer experience with all the features you need for production: hybrid static & server rendering, TypeScript support, smart bundling, route pre-fetching, and more.
desc2: With Next.js, you can build full-stack React applications. It handles the tooling and configuration needed for React, and provides additional structure, features, and optimizations for your application.
desc3: Next.js has built-in support for CSS, Sass, and CSS Modules, and you can use any CSS-in-JS library you prefer. It also supports image optimization and automatic font optimization.

title: Vue.js
titleLarge: Progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces
desc: Vue.js is a progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces. It is designed from the ground up to be incrementally adoptable.
shortDesc: Progressive framework for building modern web interfaces.
desc1: Vue is a progressive framework for building user interfaces. Unlike other monolithic frameworks, Vue is designed from the ground up to be incrementally adoptable.
desc2: The core library is focused on the view layer only, and is easy to pick up and integrate with other libraries or existing projects.
desc3: Vue is also perfectly capable of powering sophisticated Single-Page Applications when used in combination with modern tooling and supporting libraries.

title: Angular
titleLarge: Platform and framework for building single-page client applications
desc: Angular is a platform and framework for building single-page client applications using HTML and TypeScript. Angular is written in TypeScript.
shortDesc: Complete framework for building large-scale web applications.
desc1: Angular implements core and optional functionality as a set of TypeScript libraries that you import into your apps. The architecture of an Angular application relies on certain fundamental concepts.
desc2: Angular's architecture is built on the concept of components and services. Components define views, which are sets of screen elements that Angular can choose among and modify according to your program logic and data.
desc3: Angular provides built-in features for animation, http service, and materials which in turn have features like auto-complete, navigation, toolbar, menus, etc.

title: TypeScript
titleLarge: Typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript
desc: TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript. It adds static type definitions to JavaScript.
shortDesc: JavaScript with syntax for types, providing better tooling and error checking.
desc1: TypeScript extends JavaScript by adding types. By understanding JavaScript, TypeScript saves you time catching errors and providing fixes before you run code.
desc2: Any browser, any host, any OS. TypeScript compiles to JavaScript which runs anywhere JavaScript runs.
desc3: TypeScript is a language for application-scale JavaScript development. It's a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.

title: JavaScript
titleLarge: High-level programming language for web development and beyond
desc: JavaScript is a high-level, interpreted programming language that is one of the core technologies of the World Wide Web, alongside HTML and CSS.
shortDesc: The programming language that powers the modern web.
desc1: JavaScript enables interactive web pages and is an essential part of web applications. The vast majority of websites use it for client-side page behavior.
desc2: JavaScript is a multi-paradigm language, supporting event-driven, functional, and imperative programming styles. It has application programming interfaces (APIs) for working with text, dates, regular expressions, standard data structures, and the Document Object Model (DOM).
desc3: JavaScript engines were originally used only in web browsers, but they are now core components of other software systems, most notably servers and a variety of applications.

title: Python
titleLarge: High-level programming language known for its simplicity and versatility
desc: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language with dynamic semantics. Its high-level built-in data structures make it attractive for Rapid Application Development.
shortDesc: A versatile programming language for web development, data science, and automation.
desc1: Python's simple, easy to learn syntax emphasizes readability and therefore reduces the cost of program maintenance. Python supports modules and packages, which encourages program modularity and code reuse.
desc2: The Python interpreter and the extensive standard library are available in source or binary form without charge for all major platforms, and can be freely distributed.
desc3: Python is widely used in web development, data analysis, artificial intelligence, scientific computing, and automation.

title: PHP
titleLarge: Server-side scripting language designed for web development
desc: PHP is a popular general-purpose scripting language that is especially suited to web development. It is fast, flexible and pragmatic.
shortDesc: Server-side scripting language powering millions of websites.
desc1: PHP is a widely-used open source general-purpose scripting language that is especially suited for web development and can be embedded into HTML.
desc2: PHP code is usually processed on a web server by a PHP interpreter implemented as a module, a daemon or as a Common Gateway Interface (CGI) executable.
desc3: PHP has been widely ported and can be deployed on most web servers on almost every operating system and platform, free of charge.

title: Laravel
titleLarge: PHP framework for web artisans building modern applications
desc: Laravel is a web application framework with expressive, elegant syntax. It attempts to take the pain out of development by easing common tasks.
shortDesc: Elegant PHP framework for building modern web applications.
desc1: Laravel values beauty. We love clean code just as much as you do. Simple, elegant syntax puts amazing functionality at your fingertips.
desc2: Every feature has been thoughtfully considered to provide a wonderful developer experience. The Laravel ecosystem has everything you need to launch your next big idea.
desc3: Laravel has the most extensive and thorough documentation and video tutorial library of all modern web application frameworks.

title: Flutter
titleLarge: UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications from a single codebase
desc: Flutter is Google's UI toolkit for building beautiful, natively compiled applications for mobile, web, desktop, and embedded devices from a single codebase.
shortDesc: Build beautiful, natively compiled apps from a single codebase.
desc1: Flutter transforms the app development process. Build, test, and deploy beautiful mobile, web, desktop, and embedded apps from a single codebase.
desc2: Flutter's widgets incorporate all critical platform differences such as scrolling, navigation, icons and fonts to provide full native performance on both iOS and Android.
desc3: Flutter is free and open source. It's used by developers and organizations around the world. Flutter's hot reload helps you quickly and easily experiment, build UIs, add features, and fix bugs faster.

title: React Native
titleLarge: Framework for building native apps using React
desc: React Native lets you build mobile apps using only JavaScript. It uses the same design as React, letting you compose a rich mobile UI from declarative components.
shortDesc: Build native mobile apps using React and JavaScript.
desc1: React Native combines the best parts of native development with React, a best-in-class JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
desc2: Use a little—or a lot. You can use React Native today in your existing Android and iOS projects or you can create a whole new app from scratch.
desc3: React Native is being used in thousands of apps, but it's likely you've already used it in one of these apps: Facebook, Instagram, or WhatsApp.

title: Swift
titleLarge: Powerful and intuitive programming language for iOS, macOS, and more
desc: Swift is a powerful and intuitive programming language for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and watchOS. Writing Swift code is interactive and fun.
shortDesc: Apple's modern programming language for iOS and macOS development.
desc1: Swift is a robust and intuitive programming language created by Apple for building apps for iOS, Mac, Apple TV, and Apple Watch. It's designed to give developers more freedom than ever.
desc2: Swift is easy to use and open source, so anyone with an idea can create something incredible. Swift is designed to work with Apple's Cocoa and Cocoa Touch frameworks.
desc3: Swift is fast and efficient, providing real-time feedback and can be seamlessly incorporated into existing Objective-C codebases.

title: Kotlin
titleLarge: Modern programming language for Android development
desc: Kotlin is a modern, statically typed programming language used by over 60% of professional Android developers that helps boost productivity, developer satisfaction, and code safety.
shortDesc: Modern language for Android development with concise syntax.
desc1: Kotlin is a cross-platform, statically typed, general-purpose programming language with type inference. Kotlin is designed to interoperate fully with Java.
desc2: Kotlin is concise, safe, interoperable, and tool-friendly. It's used by over 60% of professional Android developers and is Google's preferred language for Android app development.
desc3: Kotlin reduces boilerplate code, prevents entire classes of errors such as null pointer exceptions, and is fully interoperable with existing Java code.

title: Ionic
titleLarge: Cross-platform mobile app development framework
desc: Ionic is a complete open-source SDK for hybrid mobile app development. Build once, run anywhere with Ionic.
shortDesc: Cross-platform framework for building mobile apps with web technologies.
desc1: Ionic is the app platform for web developers. Build amazing mobile, web, and desktop apps all with one shared code base and open-web standards.
desc2: Ionic provides a library of mobile-optimized HTML, CSS and JS components, gestures, and tools for building highly interactive apps.
desc3: Ionic is built to perform and behave great on the latest mobile devices with best practices like efficient hardware accelerated transitions, and touch-optimized gestures.

title: Xamarin
titleLarge: Cross-platform mobile app development with .NET
desc: Xamarin is a Microsoft-owned software company that provides tools for building cross-platform mobile applications using C# and .NET.
shortDesc: Build native mobile apps using C# and .NET framework.
desc1: Xamarin allows developers to build native Android, iOS, and Windows apps with a single shared .NET codebase.
desc2: Xamarin.Forms allows you to build native UIs for iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, and more using a single, shared codebase.
desc3: Xamarin provides access to platform-specific APIs and native performance, while sharing code across platforms.

title: NativeScript
titleLarge: Open-source framework for building truly native mobile apps
desc: NativeScript is an open-source framework for building truly native mobile apps with Angular, Vue.js, TypeScript, or JavaScript.
shortDesc: Build native mobile apps using web technologies.
desc1: NativeScript enables you to build truly native mobile apps from a single code base using the web skills you already have.
desc2: NativeScript uses the native rendering engine, so you get native UI and performance without WebViews.
desc3: NativeScript provides direct access to all native APIs from JavaScript, TypeScript, Angular, or Vue.js.

title: Dart
titleLarge: Client-optimized language for fast apps on any platform
desc: Dart is a client-optimized language for fast apps on any platform. It's the language behind Flutter.
shortDesc: Modern language optimized for building fast apps across platforms.
desc1: Dart is a programming language designed for client development, such as for the web and mobile apps. It is developed by Google.
desc2: Dart is an object-oriented, class-based, garbage-collected language with C-style syntax. It can compile to either native code or JavaScript.
desc3: Dart is the language used to build Flutter apps, but it can also be used standalone to build web, server, and desktop applications.

title: Java
titleLarge: Object-oriented programming language for enterprise applications
desc: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language that is designed to have as few implementation dependencies as possible.
shortDesc: Versatile programming language for enterprise and mobile development.
desc1: Java is one of the most popular programming languages used to create Web applications and platforms. It was designed for flexibility, allowing developers to write code that would run on any machine.
desc2: Java is used for building enterprise-scale applications, Android mobile apps, web applications, and embedded systems.
desc3: Java's 'write once, run anywhere' philosophy makes it a popular choice for cross-platform development.

title: C#
titleLarge: Modern, object-oriented programming language for .NET
desc: C# is a modern, object-oriented, and type-safe programming language. C# enables developers to build many types of secure and robust applications.
shortDesc: Modern language for building .NET applications across platforms.
desc1: C# is a general-purpose, multi-paradigm programming language encompassing strong typing, lexically scoped, imperative, declarative, functional, generic, object-oriented, and component-oriented programming disciplines.
desc2: C# is designed for the Common Language Infrastructure (CLI), which consists of the executable code and runtime environment that allows use of various high-level languages on different computer platforms and architectures.
desc3: C# is widely used for building Windows applications, web applications, and mobile apps with Xamarin.

title: MongoDB
titleLarge: NoSQL document database for modern applications
desc: MongoDB is a source-available cross-platform document-oriented database program. Classified as a NoSQL database program.
shortDesc: Flexible NoSQL database for modern application development.
desc1: MongoDB uses JSON-like documents with optional schemas. MongoDB is built on a scale-out architecture that has become popular with developers of all kinds.
desc2: MongoDB makes working with data easy by providing a flexible document model that maps to objects in your application code.
desc3: MongoDB is designed for scalability, performance, and high availability. It supports replication and automatic sharding.

title: PostgreSQL
titleLarge: Advanced open-source relational database system
desc: PostgreSQL is a powerful, open source object-relational database system with over 30 years of active development.
shortDesc: Advanced open-source relational database with extensive features.
desc1: PostgreSQL is a highly stable database backed by more than 20 years of development by the open-source community.
desc2: PostgreSQL is known for its proven architecture, reliability, data integrity, robust feature set, and extensibility.
desc3: PostgreSQL runs on all major operating systems and has been ACID-compliant since 2001. It has strong add-on support for commercial support as well.

title: MySQL
titleLarge: World's most popular open-source relational database
desc: MySQL is the world's most popular open-source relational database management system (RDBMS).
shortDesc: Reliable and widely-used relational database management system.
desc1: MySQL is used by many of the world's largest and fastest-growing organizations to save time and money powering their high-volume Web sites, business-critical systems, and packaged software.
desc2: MySQL is a central component of the LAMP open-source web application software stack (and other 'AMP' stacks).
desc3: MySQL is reliable, scalable, and fast. It's trusted by millions of developers and used by companies like Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube.

title: Redis
titleLarge: In-memory data structure store used as a database and cache
desc: Redis is an open-source, in-memory data structure store, used as a database, cache, and message broker.
shortDesc: Fast in-memory data store for caching and real-time applications.
desc1: Redis supports various data structures such as strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets with range queries, bitmaps, hyperloglogs, and geospatial indexes.
desc2: Redis is often used as a caching layer to improve application performance by storing frequently accessed data in memory.
desc3: Redis provides high availability via Redis Sentinel and automatic partitioning with Redis Cluster.

title: Express.js
titleLarge: Fast, unopinionated web framework for Node.js
desc: Express.js is a minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework that provides a robust set of features for web and mobile applications.
shortDesc: Fast and minimalist web framework for Node.js applications.
desc1: Express is a fast, unopinionated, minimalist web framework for Node.js. It provides a thin layer of fundamental web application features.
desc2: Express has a large ecosystem of middleware packages that can be used to add functionality to Express applications.
desc3: Express makes it easy to build robust APIs and web applications with Node.js.

title: Django
titleLarge: High-level Python web framework for rapid development
desc: Django is a high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design.
shortDesc: Batteries-included Python framework for building web applications.
desc1: Django takes care of much of the hassle of web development, so you can focus on writing your app without needing to reinvent the wheel.
desc2: Django includes dozens of extras you can use to handle common Web development tasks. Django takes care of user authentication, content administration, site maps, RSS feeds, and many more tasks.
desc3: Django follows the 'batteries included' philosophy and provides almost everything developers might want to do out of the box.

title: Firebase
titleLarge: Google's platform for building mobile and web applications
desc: Firebase is Google's mobile and web application development platform. It provides backend services and tools to help you develop high-quality apps.
shortDesc: Complete backend platform for building mobile and web apps.
desc1: Firebase provides a real-time database, authentication, cloud storage, hosting, and many other services that help you build and grow your app.
desc2: Firebase is built on Google infrastructure and scales automatically, so you can focus on building great user experiences.
desc3: Firebase integrates seamlessly with other Google services and provides SDKs for iOS, Android, and web platforms.

title: AWS
titleLarge: Amazon Web Services - Comprehensive cloud computing platform
desc: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform, offering over 200 fully featured services.
shortDesc: Leading cloud computing platform with extensive services.
desc1: AWS provides on-demand cloud computing platforms and APIs to individuals, companies, and governments, on a metered pay-as-you-go basis.
desc2: AWS offers a broad set of global cloud-based products including compute, storage, databases, analytics, networking, mobile, developer tools, management tools, IoT, security, and enterprise applications.
desc3: AWS has the largest community of customers and partners, and the most extensive network of infrastructure.

title: Docker
titleLarge: Platform for developing, shipping, and running applications in containers
desc: Docker is a platform for developing, shipping, and running applications. Docker enables you to separate your applications from your infrastructure.
shortDesc: Containerization platform for building and deploying applications.
desc1: Docker packages software into standardized units called containers that have everything the software needs to run including libraries, system tools, code, and runtime.
desc2: Docker containers are lightweight, standalone, executable packages of software that include everything needed to run an application.
desc3: Docker makes it easy to build, share, and run applications anywhere, consistently across different environments.

title: Kubernetes
titleLarge: Container orchestration platform for automating deployment and scaling
desc: Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration platform that automates deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.
shortDesc: Production-grade container orchestration for scalable applications.
desc1: Kubernetes groups containers that make up an application into logical units for easy management and discovery.
desc2: Kubernetes provides a platform for automating deployment, scaling, and operations of application containers across clusters of hosts.
desc3: Kubernetes is designed on the same principles that allow Google to run billions of containers a week.
